buyingChecks.title: Buying This X4 20i? Check These First
buyIf: * G02 (2018+) with the B48 engine (improved timing chain design)
* Post-2015 F26 with documented timing chain guide replacement
* Full service history with regular oil changes (critical for chain longevity)
* Starter motor recall work completed (2019–2021 models)
avoidIf: * Pre-2015 F26 with cold-start rattle and no chain history — assume it's overdue
* No service history — the N20 is unforgiving of neglect
* Oil leaks around the oil filter housing (belt damage risk — B48)
* Overheating history or unexplained coolant loss — cooling system may be compromised

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The X4 20i is BMW's entry-level petrol in the coupe-SUV range. The F26 (2014–2018) has the N20 — with well-known timing chain guide issues, particularly pre-2015. The G02 (2018+) has the B48 — a significant improvement with the timing chain design resolved. Common B48 issues are oil leaks, coolant leaks, and a starter motor recall. With typical X4 values ranging from £12,000 (F26) to £35,000+ (G02), an engine replacement is a significant investment — the B48-engined G02 is the safer choice.

cta.label: → Compare X4 20i engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW X4 20i (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling from the rear of the engine (N20 timing chain warning) — see the map above
* Service History — Including which engine era (N20 or B48) and oil-change discipline
* Timing Chain — For F26 N20: check for any chain work or cold-start rattle; pre-2015 most at risk
* Oil Leaks — Check around oil filter housing (B48 — belt damage risk)
* Coolant Level — Check reservoir when cold; any low or loss may indicate water pump or thermostat issues
* Recall Status — Check with BMW dealer that starter motor recall work has been completed (2019–2021 models)
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes read; unresolved issues may indicate timing, cooling or sensor problems
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures

items.question: How much does a BMW X4 20i engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A reconditioned X4 20i engine typically costs £3,500–£5,500 supply only, with the B48 variants at the higher end. Fitting adds £800–£1,300 depending on the specialist. Customers commonly report dealer estimates in the £14,000–£18,000 range for complete engine replacement, while reconditioned units are often £6,000–£8,000. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your X4.

items.question: Is the BMW X4 20i engine reliable?
items.answer: It depends entirely on which engine era your X4 20i has. The F26 (2014–2018) with the N20 has timing chain guide issues — the plastic guides can become brittle and deteriorate, causing excessive chain slack. Pre-2015 units are most at risk. The G02 (2018+) with the B48 is significantly more reliable — the timing chain design was improved. Common B48 issues include oil leaks from the oil filter housing, coolant leaks from the water pump or thermostat, and a starter motor recall on 2019–2021 models.

items.question: What are the common problems with the BMW X4 20i?
items.answer: N20 timing chain guide failure (F26 pre-2015) — rattling on cold start is the warning sign. B48 oil filter housing oil leaks and water pump/thermostat coolant leaks (G02). Starter motor recall affecting 2019–2021 models due to moisture ingress and fire risk. Carbon buildup on intake valves due to direct injection. The B48 is a significant improvement over the N20.

items.question: Is it worth replacing an X4 20i engine, or should I scrap the car?
items.answer: Depends on vehicle value and engine era. Against a well-kept G02 worth £20,000–£35,000, a £4,000–£5,000 recon is 15–25% of the car's value — worth it. Against a tired F26 worth £8,000–£12,000, the same recon may approach our 70% BMW threshold — get a scrap valuation first. B48-engined cars command a premium due to improved reliability.

items.question: How do I find my BMW X4 20i engine code?
items.answer: F26 (2014–2018): N20B20A. G02 (2018–present): B48B20A or B46B20B. Check the VIN plate or vehicle documentation.

items.question: Can I repair the N20 timing chain, or does it need replacement?
items.answer: The chain guides must be replaced — they are the weak point. A preventative replacement of the timing chain and guides costs £1,500–£3,000. A snapped chain almost always destroys the engine, requiring a £3,500–£5,500+ replacement. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately. The B48 has far fewer timing chain issues — but regular oil changes remain essential.

faq.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data from our network of engine specialists.
